title: Confessional Elements in the Poetry of Kamala Das
author: Pathode, Ganesh M.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract :-    
   Kamala Das is one of the prominent writers in Indian    literature in English. She is the most controversial and interesting female figure in the post colonial and post independence Indian English poetry. She depicted every personal experience, feelings in her poems which are universal. She raises her voice against patriarchal culture and religious orthodoxy for the modern women's voice who wants to free from all the obstacle, restrictions of Indian society. Being confessional poet, Kamala Das reveals her private life and reveals the delicate facts and even the bedroom secrets. Confessional poets have no barriers between his or her self and direct expression of that self.    Kamala Das expresses herself without any restriction and deals with the language of identity and women's identity as a woman. This present paper pays a pointed attention to the confessional tone of Kamala Das as revealed in her poetry and also        attempts to highlight the various autobiographical elements in the poetry of Kamala Das.
